name: Adam Sandler
blurb: The everyman-meathead voice of comedy rock: stand-up done with an acoustic guitar, a goofy character, and a chorus the room can sing along to by the bridge.
bio: Adam Sandler started at NYU and the Comic Strip in Manhattan, broke big on SNL (1990–1995) on the strength of song-bits — Opera Man, Lunch Lady Land, The Thanksgiving Song, and the perennial Chanukah Song — and built an audio comedy empire on the back of four Warner Bros. albums in the late 1990s (They're All Gonna Laugh at You! went double-platinum). After leaving SNL he largely abandoned the stage to run Happy Madison and headline the comedies most of his generation grew up on (Billy Madison, Happy Gilmore, The Wedding Singer, The Waterboy). His 2018 return to stand-up, the Josh Safdie-directed Netflix special 100% Fresh, was the year's best-reviewed comedy hour; he followed with 2024's Love You and the 2025 You're My Best Friend arena tour, plus a 2023 Mark Twain Prize for American Humor.

timeline.ev: Born September 9 in Brooklyn, New York
Joins Saturday Night Live as a writer; promoted to featured player and then cast
Debut comedy album They're All Gonna Laugh at You! (Warner Bros.); eventually double-platinum
Performs The Chanukah Song on SNL's Weekend Update (Dec. 3); becomes a holiday-rotation perennial
Fired from SNL alongside Chris Farley; launches the Sandman film era with Billy Madison
Netflix special 100% Fresh (directed by Steven Brill); first stand-up special; widely regarded as a career-best
Receives the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor at the Kennedy Center (March 19)
Netflix special Love You (directed by Josh Safdie); released August 27
You're My Best Friend arena tour

accolades: Mark Twain Prize for American Humor (2023)
Two Netflix stand-up specials, both directed by award-winning film directors
Independent Spirit Award; Gotham Award; multiple Razzie wins
Five seasons on Saturday Night Live (1990–1995) — Opera Man, Lunch Lady Land, Chanukah Song
Debut comedy album They're All Gonna Laugh at You! certified double-platinum

specials.t: What the Hell Happened to Me?
They're All Gonna Laugh at You!

ranking_notes: The Chanukah Song (KX5Z-HpHH9g, SNL Weekend Update 1994) is the top-100 lock — the bit that gave a generation of Jewish kids a December anthem and is the single most-quoted Sandler routine outside the films.
Chris Farley Tribute from 100% Fresh is the late-career emotional peak; almost certainly a top-100 bit on its own terms.
